stories.title: Ole Miss 4, Tennessee 1 — Lady Vols Eliminated in SEC Tournament Upset

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
UPSET ALERT 🚨

No. 13 Ole Miss just eliminated No. 5 Tennessee from the SEC Tournament.

Final: Ole Miss 4, Tennessee 1.

Two days after Karlyn Pickens went No. 1 overall in the AUSL Draft.

#SECSoftball #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Karlyn Pickens: No. 1 AUSL Draft pick on Monday.
Eliminated from the SEC Tournament on Wednesday.

That's college softball for you. Nobody is safe.

Tennessee's season is over. Ole Miss plays on. 🥎

#SECSoftball #NCAASoftball #TennesseeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
The 2026 SEC Tournament just delivered its biggest shock yet.

On Wednesday, No. 13 seed Ole Miss eliminated No. 5 Tennessee 4-1 in the second round. The Lady Vols — one of the SEC's marquee softball programs — are going home early. Tennessee had earned a first-round bye as the No. 5 seed, but Ole Miss was relentless. Pitcher Emilee Boyer had been dominant all tournament, throwing a complete-game shutout in the first round against South Carolina and then limiting Tennessee in a 4-1 statement win.

The timing makes this one sting even more for Tennessee. Karlyn Pickens — the ace who built herself into one of the most dominant pitchers in the country with a 1.44 ERA and 154 strikeouts this season — was selected No. 1 overall in the AUSL Draft by the Carolina Blaze just two days before the loss. Her college career ends not with a championship run, but with an upset elimination.

Ole Miss advances to the quarterfinals. Tennessee heads home.

Does this change where you think Tennessee lands in the NCAA Tournament bracket? Should they still be a top-16 national seed?
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
No. 13 Ole Miss eliminates No. 5 Tennessee 4-1 at the SEC Tournament. Karlyn Pickens' college career is over. 🥎
```

stories.title: Auburn 11, Texas A&M 8 — Tigers Stun Aggies With 5 HRs, Ratliff Walk-Off

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Auburn went 4-20 in SEC play this season.

Then they went to the SEC Tournament and hit FIVE HOME RUNS against Texas A&M.

Final: Auburn 11, A&M 8.

Ava Ratliff — 3-run walk-off in the 7th. 🔥

#AuburnSoftball #SECSoftball #NCAASoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Two outs. Bottom — wait, TOP of the 7th. Game tied at 8.

Freshman Ava Ratliff steps up.

THREE-RUN HOME RUN. GAME OVER.

Auburn stuns Texas A&M 11-8 at the SEC Tournament. 🥎

#AuburnSoftball #SECSoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
You cannot write a better tournament storyline than this.

Auburn finished the 2026 SEC regular season at 4-20 in conference play — a brutal stretch that left the Tigers far outside the at-large conversation for the NCAA Tournament. But come tournament time in Lexington? Auburn showed up as a completely different team.

On Wednesday against Texas A&M, Auburn hit five home runs. McKaela Walker, AnnaLea Adams, Ma'Nia Womack, and Haven Roebuck all went deep. The Aggies clawed back to tie the game at 8-8 across the fifth and sixth innings — and then freshman Ava Ratliff stepped to the plate in the top of the seventh with two outs and the game on the line.

Three-run home run. Ballgame. Final: Auburn 11, Texas A&M 8.

For A&M, this loss is genuinely painful. Their hopes for hosting an NCAA Regional take a hit when the selection committee reviews the bracket. For Auburn, this is the kind of moment that defines a team's year — regardless of what came before it.

Auburn faces No. 3 Florida in the quarterfinals. The Tigers are not done yet.

Who wins: Auburn or Florida? 🐯🐊
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
Auburn (4-20 in conference) just beat Texas A&M 11-8 with FIVE home runs. Ava Ratliff's walk-off 3-run shot in the 7th ended it. 🔥
```

stories.title: Oklahoma SEC Quarterfinal Preview — No. 1 Sooners vs. Georgia Tonight at 7 PM CT

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
No. 1 Oklahoma (48-7) opens the SEC Tournament quarterfinals TONIGHT.

Opponent: No. 9 Georgia — who just knocked off LSU 7-3.

Time: 7 PM CT | Lexington, KY

The Sooners are the top seed. Tournament time. 🥎

#BoomerSooner #SECSoftball #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
The No. 1 team in the country takes the field tonight — and it matters.

Oklahoma (48-7) enters the 2026 SEC Tournament quarterfinals as the top overall seed after winning the SEC Regular Season Championship with a 20-4 conference record. The Sooners earned a bye through the first two rounds. Tonight, they face No. 9 Georgia at 7 PM CT at John Cropp Stadium in Lexington, Kentucky.

Georgia got here by beating No. 8 LSU 7-3 in the first round. They are not a pushover. But this is still Oklahoma — the team that has been the country's No. 1 squad for the past four weeks running.

The storyline here is simple: Can anyone slow Oklahoma down as we approach the NCAA Tournament? The Sooners are built for this — deep lineup, proven pitching, and the experience of having navigated the SEC in their second year. Tonight is their first real test in Lexington.

Watch at 7 PM CT. This is what tournament softball is all about.

Do you think Oklahoma runs the table in Lexington?
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
No. 1 Oklahoma opens SEC Tournament quarterfinals tonight vs. Georgia — 7 PM CT. Sooners are the top overall seed. 🥎 #BoomerSooner
```

stories.title: Nebraska 18-Game Win Streak — Huskers Enter Big Ten Tournament as No. 1 Seed

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Nebraska: 18-game win streak.
Nebraska: First-ever Big Ten Regular Season Champions.
Nebraska: No. 2 nationally. 43-6 on the season.

The Huskers open Big Ten Tournament play TODAY as the No. 1 seed.

Is Nebraska a WCWS darkhorse? 👀

#Huskers #BigTenSoftball #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
Let's talk about the hottest team in college softball right now.

Nebraska has won 18 consecutive games. That's the longest active win streak in Division I softball — and the second-longest in Huskers program history. Over this stretch, Nebraska (43-6) claimed the program's first-ever Big Ten Regular Season Championship, tied the Big Ten record for most conference wins in a single season, and climbed to No. 2 in the NFCA Top 25.

Now they enter the Big Ten Tournament as the No. 1 seed in Bloomington. Nebraska has a first-round bye and opens Thursday in the quarterfinals. If you haven't been watching the Huskers this spring, it's time to start.

The conversation around this team is real: Nebraska is not just a Big Ten power — they are a legitimate WCWS contender. An 18-game win streak at the end of the regular season is the kind of momentum that carries programs deep into the postseason.

Can Nebraska make a run at Oklahoma City? 🌽
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
Nebraska: 18-game win streak. First-ever Big Ten Champs. No. 2 nationally. The Huskers open the Big Ten Tournament TODAY as the top seed. 🌽
```

stories.title: Texas Tech 50-5 — First Team in D-I to 50 Wins Plays Big 12 Tournament Today

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech has 50 wins.

That's the first D-I team to reach 50 in 2026.

Record: 50-5. Big 12 Regular Season Champs. No. 3 in the country.

Canady threw a perfect game on Senior Day.

Today: No. 1 seed in the Big 12 Tournament. 🔴⚫

#TexasTechSoftball #Big12Softball #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
Fifty wins. In May.

Texas Tech is the first program in Division I softball to reach 50 wins in 2026 — finishing the regular season at 50-5 with a second consecutive Big 12 Regular Season Championship. At No. 3 nationally, the Red Raiders have been one of the three best teams in the country all year long.

The story of Texas Tech's season is built around elite pitching. Ace Canady — already one of the nation's premier arms — threw a complete-game perfect game on Senior Day last Saturday (May 2). That kind of dominance from your staff's best arm heading into the postseason is as encouraging a sign as any.

Texas Tech enters the Big 12 Tournament as the No. 1 seed and defending champion, facing No. 8 Baylor today at 1:30 PM CT at Devon Park in Oklahoma City. If the Red Raiders run the table this week, they'll be one of the top national seeds when the NCAA Tournament bracket drops.

Can Texas Tech complete the season with a Big 12 title and a deep NCAA Tournament run?
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
Texas Tech is the first D-I team to reach 50 wins in 2026. 50-5. Big 12 Champs. Big 12 Tournament No. 1 seed. Today: vs. Baylor. 🔴⚫
```

stories.title: NFCA Week 14 Rankings — Oklahoma No. 1, Nebraska No. 2, Texas Tech No. 3

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
NFCA Top 25 — Week 14:

No. 1 Oklahoma (48-7) — 4th straight week
No. 2 Nebraska (43-6)
No. 3 Texas Tech (50-5)
No. 4 Alabama

Conference tournament week. The board is about to get shuffled. 🥎

#NCAASoftball #NFCARankings #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
The NFCA Week 14 Coaches Poll is out — and the top of the rankings looks like a preview of what matters come Oklahoma City.

No. 1: Oklahoma (48-7) — fourth consecutive week at the top
No. 2: Nebraska (43-6) — riding an 18-game win streak into the Big Ten Tournament
No. 3: Texas Tech (50-5) — first team in D-I to 50 wins this year
No. 4: Alabama

These rankings reflect games through May 3 — so conference tournament results this week will shake things up before the NCAA Tournament bracket is revealed. Tennessee's second-round SEC Tournament loss will raise questions about their seeding. Alabama's position could shift depending on their run in Lexington.

Bookmark this: The 2026 Women's College World Series is coming, and the teams above are the ones most likely to see you there.

Where do you see Oklahoma, Nebraska, and Texas Tech landing in the NCAA Tournament bracket?
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
NFCA Week 14: Oklahoma No. 1 (4th week), Nebraska No. 2, Texas Tech No. 3. Conference tournament week will shake it all up. 🥎
```
